data.title: WhatConverts Tips: Best Ways to Track Every Lead
data.excerpt: Discover the best WhatConverts tips to track calls, forms, and chats. See which campaigns drive real leads and stop wasting ad spend today.

$media.alt: WhatConverts Tips: Best Ways to Track Every Lead

children.text: *Disclosure: This post contains affiliate links. If you sign up through our links, we may earn a commission at no extra cost to you.*
Why Most Marketers Are Flying Blind (And How WhatConverts Fixes That)

Here is a uncomfortable truth: most businesses spend thousands on ads every month and have no clear idea which campaigns actually bring in paying customers. They look at clicks, impressions, maybe even form submissions — but the moment a lead picks up the phone, they are guessing.

I was in that exact spot two years ago. Running Google Ads for a local service business, I could see which keywords got clicks. But calls? Walk-ins from a landing page visit three days earlier? Total mystery.
That changed when I found
WhatConverts

. It is a marketing attribution platform that ties every lead — calls, forms, chats, even transactions — back to the exact campaign, keyword, and ad that generated it. No more guessing. No more spreadsheets trying to match phone numbers to ad groups.
If you want to see it in action,
check out WhatConverts here
. The setup is faster than you would expect.
What Exactly Does WhatConverts Do?
At its core, WhatConverts answers one question: *where are my leads coming from?*
But it goes deeper than a simple source report. Here is what the platform actually tracks:
Phone calls
— Dynamic number insertion swaps phone numbers on your site based on the visitor's traffic source. Every call gets attributed to the right campaign.
Form submissions
— Whether it is a contact form, quote request, or newsletter signup, each one is logged with full attribution data.
Chats
— Live chat conversations are captured and attributed the same way.
Transactions and revenue
— If you close leads in a CRM, WhatConverts can pull revenue data back into its reports so you see not just leads, but *valuable* leads.
The dashboard gives you a single view of every lead with details like caller ID, landing page, keyword, session duration, and even call recordings. You can filter, sort, and export everything.
Best Ways to Set Up WhatConverts for Maximum Impact
Getting the tool running is straightforward, but a few setup decisions make a massive difference in data quality. Here are the tips that worked best for me:
1. Use Dynamic Number Insertion From Day One

This is the feature that makes call tracking actually work. Instead of showing one phone number to everyone, WhatConverts dynamically serves a different number based on how the visitor arrived — organic search, paid ad, social, referral, whatever.

When you set this up correctly, every single phone call gets tagged with the source, medium, campaign, keyword, and landing page. I recommend installing the tracking script on every page that displays a phone number, not just your homepage.
2. Create Lead Quoting Rules Early

Not every lead is equal. A spam form submission is not the same as a five-minute phone call from someone ready to buy. WhatConverts lets you set up "lead quoting" rules that automatically score or categorize leads based on duration, form fields, or other criteria.

For example, I set a rule that any call under 30 seconds is marked as "not a lead." Any form submission with a real business email gets tagged as "qualified." This filters the noise before it clutters your reports.
3. Connect Your CRM or E-Commerce Platform
The real magic happens when you close the loop between marketing and revenue. WhatConverts integrates with platforms like
monday.com
ClickUp
, and dozens of CRMs. When a lead converts into a paying customer, that revenue data flows back into WhatConverts.
Now your reports show not just cost-per-lead, but cost-per-acquisition and return on ad spend by keyword. That is the data that actually helps you make budget decisions.
4. Set Up Custom Lead Fields for Form Tracking

By default, WhatConverts captures standard form fields. But if your forms include custom fields — like project budget, service type, or location — you can map those into the platform. This gives you richer lead profiles and lets you segment reports by the attributes that matter to your business.
I mapped a "Service Needed" dropdown into WhatConverts and was shocked to discover that 40% of our ad spend was driving leads for a service we did not even offer anymore. Fixed that in two days.
5. Use the Keyword-Level Attribution Daily

The WhatConverts dashboard lets you drill down to the exact keyword that generated each lead. I make it a habit to check this view every morning. It takes five minutes and helps me spot winning keywords to scale and losers to pause — way faster than waiting for Google Ads to optimize itself.
If you are serious about squeezing more from your ad budget,
this is the tool that makes it possible
. The keyword data alone pays for the subscription within weeks.
Notable Features That Set WhatConverts Apart
WhatConverts keeps adding features that make the platform more powerful. Here are some standouts worth knowing about (you can read more in our
detailed feature breakdown
Lead Intelligence Reports

— Instead of just showing raw lead counts, WhatConverts now surfaces patterns. It highlights which sources, pages, and times of day produce your highest-quality leads. You get actionable insights without digging through data yourself.
Transcription and AI Summaries

— Call recordings are automatically transcribed, and the platform generates concise summaries. No more listening to 20-minute calls to figure out if a lead was legitimate. The AI does the heavy lifting and you review in seconds.
Multi-Touch Attribution Models

— If a customer clicks a Google Ad, leaves, then comes back three days later through an organic search and calls, which channel gets credit? WhatConverts supports multiple attribution models — first touch, last touch, and everything in between — so you can see the full customer journey.
White-Label Reporting for Agencies

— If you manage marketing for clients, WhatConverts lets you brand reports with your agency logo and colors. Clients see clean, professional dashboards without knowing the underlying tool. This alone is a retention tool.
Spam and Robocall Filtering
— The platform detects and filters out spam calls automatically. Your lead counts stay accurate and your team does not waste time on junk calls.
**Integration with
Apollo.io

** and other sales tools — Leads captured in WhatConverts can be pushed directly into your outbound stack. Imagine seeing which inbound leads match your ideal customer profile in Apollo before you even follow up.
Tips for Getting the Most Out of Your Dashboard
The WhatConverts dashboard is powerful, but only if you know how to use it. A few habits that made a big difference for me:
Customize your default view.
The default dashboard shows all leads. Set up filtered views for "qualified leads only" or "calls over 2 minutes" so you see high-signal data first.
Export weekly summaries.

WhatConverts can email you a weekly report with lead counts by source, top-performing keywords, and cost-per-lead. Set this up once and you get a performance snapshot in your inbox every Monday without logging in.
Tag leads manually when needed.

Sometimes you know a lead is gold the moment you see it — a call from a Fortune 500 company, a form submission with a massive project scope. Tag those leads manually so they surface in your best-lead reports later.
Compare date ranges side by side.

The comparison tool lets you see this month vs. last month, or this quarter vs. the same quarter last year. Trend data is more useful than single snapshots, especially when presenting results to a boss or client.
Who Is WhatConverts Ideal For?
WhatConverts is not trying to be everything for everyone. It solves a specific, expensive problem. Here is who benefits the most:
Local service businesses.
Plumbers, lawyers, dentists, HVAC companies — any business where the phone is the primary conversion channel. If half your leads call instead of filling out a form, you need call attribution. Period.
Marketing agencies managing paid ads.

If you run Google Ads or Meta Ads for clients and need to prove ROI, WhatConverts gives you lead-level proof. Not vague "impressions" or "engagement" metrics. Actual leads with names, numbers, and revenue attached.
E-commerce brands with high-consideration products.
If your average order value is high and customers often call before buying, tracking those calls back to ad campaigns closes a gap that standard analytics platforms leave wide open.
B2B companies with long sales cycles.
When a lead takes weeks to convert, multi-touch attribution matters. WhatConverts tracks the journey so you know which touchpoints actually moved the needle.
Solo marketers and consultants.

You do not need a team to use it. The interface is clean, the setup wizard walks you through the basics, and the reporting is clear enough to share with a client or stakeholder without spending hours formatting spreadsheets.
If you fall into any of these categories,
give WhatConverts a try
. The insights it surfaces will change how you allocate your marketing budget.
Common Mistakes to Avoid When Using WhatConverts
A few pitfalls I have seen (and experienced myself) when getting started:
Not tracking enough numbers.
If you only assign one tracking number per campaign, you will miss attribution on calls that happen after a visitor browses multiple pages. Use dynamic number insertion across your whole site.
Ignoring form tracking.
Call tracking gets most of the attention, but forms generate plenty of leads too. Make sure your WhatConverts script is installed on every form, including embedded third-party forms and pop-ups.
Skipping the qualification rules.
Without lead scoring, your reports will be noisy. A 10-second wrong-number call will inflate your lead counts and skew your cost-per-lead calculations. Set up duration-based rules on day one.
Not connecting revenue data.
Leads are nice. Revenue is better. Even a simple CSV upload of closed deals lets WhatConverts calculate true ROAS. Do not leave that on the table.
For a step-by-step walkthrough of the full setup process, check out our
complete WhatConverts tutorial
Final Thoughts: Stop Guessing, Start Knowing

Marketing attribution is not a luxury. It is the difference between scaling what works and burning budget on what does not. WhatConverts makes attribution accessible — not just for enterprise teams with six-figure analytics budgets, but for small businesses and agencies that need answers now.

The platform tracks calls, forms, and chats with precision. The dashboard is clean. The integrations are solid. And the pricing scales with your lead volume, so you are not overpaying when you are small.

I have recommended WhatConverts to more colleagues than I can count. Every single one came back within a month saying some version of: "I had no idea that campaign was wasting money" or "I found a keyword that is converting at half the cost."
That is the value. Clear data, better decisions, less waste.
If you are ready to see exactly where your leads come from,
start with WhatConverts today
. You will wonder how you ever made budget decisions without it.

children.text: What types of leads can WhatConverts track?

WhatConverts tracks phone calls, form submissions, live chats, and e-commerce transactions. Each lead is automatically attributed to its marketing source, including the specific campaign, keyword, and landing page that generated it.
Is WhatConverts suitable for small businesses or just agencies?

Both. WhatConverts works well for small local businesses that rely on phone calls and for agencies managing multiple client accounts. The pricing scales with lead volume, and the interface is simple enough for solo marketers to use without training.
How does WhatConverts handle call tracking attribution?

WhatConverts uses dynamic number insertion to assign unique phone numbers based on each visitor's traffic source. When someone calls, the platform records the source, campaign, keyword, and landing page, giving you full attribution data for every phone lead.
Can WhatConverts integrate with my existing CRM?

Yes. WhatConverts integrates with popular CRMs and project management tools like monday.com, ClickUp, Salesforce, HubSpot, and many others. You can push lead data into your workflow and pull revenue data back into WhatConverts for true ROI reporting.
